```python
from docx.shared import Pt

print("Document paragraphs:", len(doc.paragraphs))
print("\nSearching for italicized text...")

italic_count = 0
for para_idx, para in enumerate(doc.paragraphs):
    for run_idx, run in enumerate(para.runs):
        if run.italic:
            italic_count += 1

            print(f"  Current font size: {run.font.size}")
            print(f"  Italic: {run.italic}")

print(f"\nTotal italic runs found: {italic_count}")
```

```python
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Pt

# Load the document
doc_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_osworld_e246f6d8_7no2c01k/Y22-2119-assign4.docx"
doc = Document(doc_path)

# Change font size of all italicized text to 14pt
modified_count = 0
for para in doc.paragraphs:
    for run in para.runs:
        if run.italic:
            run.font.size = Pt(14)
            modified_count += 1

print(f"Modified {modified_count} italic runs to 14pt")

# Save the document
doc.save(doc_path)
print(f"Document saved to: {doc_path}")

# Verify the changes
doc = Document(doc_path)
print("\nVerifying changes...")
for para_idx, para in enumerate(doc.paragraphs):
    for run_idx, run in enumerate(para.runs):
        if run.italic:
            print(f"Para {para_idx}, Run {run_idx}: '{run.text[:30]}...' - Font size: {run.font.size}")
```

```python
from docx import Document
import re

# Load the document
doc_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_osworld_88fe4b2d_0kuao9k_/CCCH9003_Tutorial_guidelines.docx"
doc = Document(doc_path)

# Get the first paragraph
first_para = doc.paragraphs[0]
original_text = first_para.text

# Split into sentences (handle periods, exclamation marks)
sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', original_text.strip())
sentences = [s.strip() for s in sentences if s.strip()]

print(f"Found {len(sentences)} sentences:")
for i, s in enumerate(sentences):
    print(f"  {i}: {s}")

# Clear the first paragraph and add the first sentence
first_para.clear()
first_para.add_run(sentences[0])

# Get the parent element to insert new paragraphs
parent = first_para._element.getparent()

# Insert remaining sentences with empty lines between them
# Insert in reverse order to maintain correct position
insert_index = 1  # Position after first paragraph

for sentence in sentences[1:]:
    # Insert empty paragraph first
    empty_p = doc.add_paragraph()
    parent.insert(insert_index, empty_p._element)
    insert_index += 1
    
    # Insert sentence paragraph
    new_p = doc.add_paragraph(sentence)
    parent.insert(insert_index, new_p._element)
    insert_index += 1

# Save the document
doc.save(doc_path)
print(f"\nDocument saved to: {doc_path}")

# Verify the changes
doc2 = Document(doc_path)
print("\nFirst 15 paragraphs after modification:")
for i, para in enumerate(doc2.paragraphs[:15]):
    print(f"  {i}: '{para.text}'")
```

Full document content:
Para 0: “Seeing is believing” seems self-evident. That is why we even question our own sanity if we cannot trust our eyes. Yet vision is neither innate nor inborn. It is developed in early children by input of clear images from each eye. Through our vision, as well as other faculties, children develop a schematic concept of the world. For this reason, we are surprised when our preconceived ideas are challenged, as when we are presented with optical illusions (e.g. Escher perpetual steps). The process of perception involves receiving images, analysing them and making predictions based on memory, expectations and previous experience. When the brain function is disturbed, we hallucinate and see things that are not there. Stated simply, the first objective of this course is to demonstrate that we see with our brains and not with our eyes. We will do this by exploring the physics, anatomy and physiology of vision including functional MRI imaging of the vision of the brain when presented with different images. 

Para 1: If our perception can be influenced by our experience, the reverse can also apply. By manipulating images, we can create different perceptions. “Photographs don’t lie.'' is another truism. Images can be manipulated. Throughout history, there are also many examples of mass manipulation from propaganda images. Today with the widespread use of digital photography and the use of multi-media in journalism, computer manipulation is made easier. There are many examples of politicians being airbrushed out or juxtaposed. Simply cropping photographs can portray a different story. Our second objective is to help students appreciate our perceptions which can be influenced by the manipulation of images. They can actively participate by finding everyday examples from history, from journalism, from advertisement and analyse the devices used.

Para 2: “Beauty is in the eye of the beholder”. Is beauty entirely subjective? Is our judgement of how attractive a member of the opposite sex influenced by our own perception of how good looking we are? We are going to approach our individual preferences and the prevailing concept of beauty from the point of view of the development of self-image, of psychosexual growth of young adolescence. The objective is to see if we could challenge students to take a more detached look at the prevailing culture of body image.

```python
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import RGBColor

# Load the document
doc_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_osworld_8472fece_l26aeds9/Dolch_Sight_Words_Primer.docx"
doc = Document(doc_path)

# Define colors
RED = RGBColor(255, 0, 0)
BLUE = RGBColor(0, 0, 255)
VOWELS = {'A', 'E', 'I', 'O', 'U', 'a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u'}

# Process all tables
word_count = 0
vowel_words = 0
consonant_words = 0

for table in doc.tables:
    for row in table.rows:
        for cell in row.cells:
            text = cell.text.strip()
            if text and ' ' not in text:  # Single word cells only
                word_count += 1
                # Check first letter
                first_char = text[0]
                if first_char in VOWELS:
                    color = RED
                    vowel_words += 1
                    print(f"RED (vowel): {text}")
                else:
                    color = BLUE
                    consonant_words += 1
                    print(f"BLUE (consonant): {text}")
                
                # Apply color to all runs in the cell
                for paragraph in cell.paragraphs:
                    for run in paragraph.runs:
                        run.font.color.rgb = color

print(f"\nTotal words processed: {word_count}")
print(f"Vowel words (red): {vowel_words}")
print(f"Consonant words (blue): {consonant_words}")

# Save the document
doc.save(doc_path)
print(f"\nDocument saved to: {doc_path}")
```

Details:
- **Unique Slide Titles:** Each slide must have a unique, machine-readable title for screen readers. This is typically done by adding a text box with a descriptive title and placing it off the visible slide area. The titles must be distinct for each slide.
- **Logical Reading Order:** The reading order of elements on each slide must be logical for a screen reader. The sequence should generally follow a natural flow, such as the slide's main title first, followed by content read from top-to-bottom and left-to-right.
- **Theme Font Consistency:** All text throughout the presentation must use the theme's default font. Any direct font family overrides (e.g., 'DM Sans', 'Arial') must be removed, while preserving all other text formatting like size, color, and weight (bold/italic).
- **Visual Preservation:** The visual layout, content, and positioning of on-screen elements must not be altered. The only acceptable visual change is the font family reverting to the theme's default.

```python
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Pt, Inches, Emu
from pptx.dml.color import RGBColor
from pptx.enum.shapes import MSO_SHAPE_TYPE
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN
from pptx.oxml.ns import qn
from pptx.oxml import parse_xml
import copy

# Load the presentation
path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_67_wcag_accessibility_master_cleanup_nb6bcv_a/case_67_wcag_accessibility_master_cleanup_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(path)

# Slide titles for accessibility (unique for each slide)
slide_titles = [
    "Five Ideas Infographic",
    "Data Table", 
    "Six Ideas Infographic",
    "Five Main Points Infographic",
    "Resource Page",
    "Credits Page"
]

# Function to remove font family override (set to None to use theme default)
def remove_font_override(shape):
    if not shape.has_text_frame:
        return
    for para in shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
        for run in para.runs:
            # Remove direct font family override by setting name to None
            # This will use the theme's default font
            if run.font.name is not None:
                run.font.name = None

# Function to process shapes recursively (including groups)
def process_shape_fonts(shape):
    if shape.shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE.GROUP:
        for s in shape.shapes:
            process_shape_fonts(s)
    else:
        remove_font_override(shape)

# Process each slide
for i, slide in enumerate(prs.slides):
    print(f"\nProcessing Slide {i+1}: {slide_titles[i]}")
    
    # 1. Add unique off-screen title for screen readers
    # Place it above the slide (negative y position) so it's not visible
    title_box = slide.shapes.add_textbox(
        Inches(0.5), Inches(-1),  # x=0.5", y=-1" (off-screen above)
        Inches(9), Inches(0.5)    # width=9", height=0.5"
    )
    tf = title_box.text_frame
    tf.text = slide_titles[i]
    p = tf.paragraphs[0]
    p.alignment = PP_ALIGN.LEFT
    # Make the title accessible but not visible (white text on white, or just off-screen)
    # Actually, placing it off-screen is sufficient
    
    # 2. Remove font overrides from all shapes
    font_count = 0
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        # Skip the title we just added
        if shape == title_box:
            continue
            
        # Check if shape has direct font override
        if shape.has_text_frame:
            for para in shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
                for run in para.runs:
                    if run.font.name is not None:
                        font_count += 1
                        run.font.name = None
        
        # Process groups recursively
        if shape.shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE.GROUP:
            process_shape_fonts(shape)
    
    print(f"  - Added off-screen title: '{slide_titles[i]}'")
    print(f"  - Processed fonts in {len(slide.shapes)} shapes")
```

```python
from pptx.chart.data import ChartData
from pptx.enum.chart import XL_CHART_TYPE
import re

# Load the presentation
path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_29_convert_bar_chart_to_pie_chart_furmwc80/case_29_convert_bar_chart_to_pie_chart_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(path)

# Examine slide 3 (index 2)
slide = prs.slides[2]

# Find the chart shape
for shape in slide.shapes:
    if shape.has_chart:
        chart = shape.chart
        
        # Get the chart space XML
        from lxml import etree
        chart_space = chart._chartSpace
        xml_str = etree.tostring(chart_space, pretty_print=True, encoding='unicode')
        
        # Look for all category values in the cat section
        # Find the cat element and extract all pt values
        cat_section = re.search(r'<c:cat>(.*?)</c:cat>', xml_str, re.DOTALL)
        if cat_section:
            cat_content = cat_section.group(1)
            # Find all <c:v> values within the cat section
            cat_values = re.findall(r'<c:v>([^<]+)</c:v>', cat_content)
            print(f"All categories: {cat_values}")
        
        # Also get series values
        for series in chart.series:
            print(f"Series values: {series.values}")
            print(f"Number of values: {len(series.values)}")
            
        # Store position info
        print(f"Position: left={shape.left}, top={shape.top}, width={shape.width}, height={shape.height}")
        
        # Store for later use
        chart_shape = shape
        old_left = shape.left
        old_top = shape.top
        old_width = shape.width
        old_height = shape.height
        series_values = list(series.values)
        series_name = series.name
```

```python
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt, Emu
from pptx.chart.data import ChartData
from pptx.enum.chart import XL_CHART_TYPE

# Load the presentation
path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_29_convert_bar_chart_to_pie_chart_furmwc80/case_29_convert_bar_chart_to_pie_chart_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(path)

# Get slide 3 (index 2)
slide = prs.slides[2]

# Find the chart shape and get its data
chart_shape = None
for shape in slide.shapes:
    if shape.has_chart:
        chart_shape = shape
        chart = shape.chart
        
        # Get position
        old_left = shape.left
        old_top = shape.top
        old_width = shape.width
        old_height = shape.height
        
        # Get data from the chart
        for series in chart.series:
            series_values = list(series.values)
            series_name = series.name
        
        # Get categories from XML
        from lxml import etree
        import re
        chart_space = chart._chartSpace
        xml_str = etree.tostring(chart_space, pretty_print=True, encoding='unicode')
        cat_section = re.search(r'<c:cat>(.*?)</c:cat>', xml_str, re.DOTALL)
        if cat_section:
            cat_content = cat_section.group(1)
            categories = re.findall(r'<c:v>([^<]+)</c:v>', cat_content)
        
        print(f"Found chart at position: left={old_left}, top={old_top}, width={old_width}, height={old_height}")
        print(f"Categories: {categories}")
        print(f"Values: {series_values}")
        print(f"Series name: {series_name}")
        break

# Remove the old chart
if chart_shape:
    sp = chart_shape._element
    sp.getparent().remove(sp)
    print("Old chart removed")

# Create new chart data for pie chart
chart_data = ChartData()
chart_data.categories = categories
chart_data.add_series(series_name, series_values)

# Add the new pie chart
new_chart = slide.shapes.add_chart(
    XL_CHART_TYPE.PIE,
    old_left,
    old_top,
    old_width,
    old_height,
    chart_data
).chart

print(f"New pie chart added at same position")

# Save the presentation
prs.save(path)
print(f"Presentation saved to {path}")
```

```python
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt, Emu
from pptx.enum.chart import XL_DATA_LABEL_POSITION
from pptx.enum.shapes import MSO_SHAPE_TYPE
from lxml import etree

path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_73_dynamic_data_label_placement_n2s1lyjd/case_73_dynamic_data_label_placement_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(path)
slide = prs.slides[0]

# Get the chart shape
chart_shape = slide.shapes[12]
chart = chart_shape.chart

print("Fixing data labels...")
# Fix data labels - set to OUTSIDE_END to avoid overlap
for series in chart.series:
    if hasattr(series, 'data_labels') and series.data_labels:
        # Set label position to OUTSIDE_END
        series.data_labels.position = XL_DATA_LABEL_POSITION.OUTSIDE_END
        # Enable showing values
        series.data_labels.show_value = True
        print(f"  Set {series.name} labels to OUTSIDE_END")

print("\nRepositioning metric boxes...")

# Metric boxes are shapes 4-11 (4 backgrounds + 4 text boxes)
# Current positions: shapes 4,6,8,10 are backgrounds; 5,7,9,11 are text boxes
# They are at right side: x=8.8 to 10.8, y=0.9 to 1.8

# New layout: place them in a horizontal row above the chart
# Starting from x=3.6 (chart left), y=1.1 (chart top - some space)
# Each box: width ~1.8, height ~0.75, with spacing

# Define new positions for metric boxes (horizontal row)
box_width = Inches(1.8)
box_height = Inches(0.75)
start_x = Inches(3.6)  # Align with chart left
start_y = Inches(1.1)  # Above chart
spacing = Inches(0.2)  # Space between boxes

# Metric box pairs: (background, text)
metric_boxes = [
    (4, 5),   # Total Rev
    (6, 7),   # YoY
    (8, 9),   # NPS
    (10, 11)  # Churn
]

for idx, (bg_idx, txt_idx) in enumerate(metric_boxes):
    new_x = start_x + idx * (box_width + spacing)
    new_y = start_y
    
    # Move background shape
    bg_shape = slide.shapes[bg_idx]
    bg_shape.left = new_x
    bg_shape.top = new_y
    bg_shape.width = box_width
    bg_shape.height = box_height
    
    # Move text box
    txt_shape = slide.shapes[txt_idx]
    txt_shape.left = new_x + Inches(0.1)  # Slight offset for text
    txt_shape.top = new_y + Inches(0.06)
    txt_shape.width = box_width - Inches(0.2)
    txt_shape.height = box_height - Inches(0.15)
    
    print(f"  Moved box {idx+1} to ({new_x.inches:.2f}, {new_y.inches:.2f})")

print("\nResizing and repositioning chart...")

# Move chart below the metric boxes
chart_new_y = start_y + box_height + Inches(0.3)  # Below boxes with margin
chart_new_height = Inches(7.5) - chart_new_y - Inches(0.3)  # Fill remaining space

chart_shape.top = chart_new_y
chart_shape.height = chart_new_height
# Keep same width and left position
chart_shape.left = Inches(3.6)
chart_shape.width = Inches(9.2)

print(f"  Chart moved to y={chart_new_y.inches:.2f}, height={chart_new_height.inches:.2f}")

# Save the presentation
prs.save(path)
print(f"\nSaved to: {path}")
```

```python
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Pt, Inches, Emu
from pptx.dml.color import RGBColor
from pptx.enum.action import PP_ACTION
from pptx.oxml.ns import qn
from pptx.oxml import parse_xml

# Load the presentation
path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_59_convert_hyper_link_sbtehkm9/case_59_convert_hyper_link_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(path)

# Get the text box with the URLs (Shape 10 on Slide 1)
slide = prs.slides[0]
shape = slide.shapes[10]

# Get the paragraph and clear it
text_frame = shape.text_frame
paragraph = text_frame.paragraphs[0]

# Store original font properties
original_run = paragraph.runs[0]
original_font_size = original_run.font.size
original_font_name = original_run.font.name

# Clear the paragraph
paragraph.clear()

# Define the text segments
segments = [
    ("Visit ", None),
    ("https://www.berkeley.edu/", "https://www.berkeley.edu/"),
    (" and ", None),
    ("https://illinois.edu/", "https://illinois.edu/"),
    (" for more details", None)
]

# Add runs with hyperlinks
for text, hyperlink_url in segments:
    run = paragraph.add_run()
    run.text = text
    run.font.size = original_font_size
    if original_font_name:
        run.font.name = original_font_name
    
    if hyperlink_url:
        # Add hyperlink
        run.hyperlink.address = hyperlink_url

# Verify the changes
print("Modified text:")
for run_idx, run in enumerate(paragraph.runs):
    hyperlink = run.hyperlink.address if run.hyperlink else None
    print(f"  Run {run_idx}: '{run.text}' -> hyperlink: {hyperlink}")

# Save the presentation
prs.save(path)
print(f"\nSaved to: {path}")
```

```python
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches
from pptx.enum.shapes import MSO_SHAPE_TYPE

# Load the presentation
path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_27_correct_images_3ljtosfh/case_27_correct_images_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(path)

slide = prs.slides[0]

# Map pictures and text boxes by their horizontal positions
pictures = {}
text_boxes = {}

for shape in slide.shapes:
    if 'Picture' in shape.name or '그림' in shape.name:
        # Determine position by left coordinate
        left = shape.left.inches
        if left < 4:  # Leftmost
            pictures['left'] = shape
        elif left < 7:  # Center
            pictures['center'] = shape
        else:  # Right
            pictures['right'] = shape
    elif shape.has_text_frame and shape.text_frame.text.strip():
        text = shape.text_frame.text.strip()
        if text in ['shark', 'car', 'baseball']:
            left = shape.left.inches
            if left < 4:  # Leftmost - shark
                text_boxes['shark'] = shape
            elif left < 7:  # Center - car
                text_boxes['car'] = shape
            else:  # Right - baseball
                text_boxes['baseball'] = shape

print("Current state:")
for pos in ['left', 'center', 'right']:
    pic = pictures[pos]
    print(f"{pos} picture: {pic.name}, size={pic.width.inches:.2f}x{pic.height.inches:.2f}, top={pic.top.inches:.2f}")

for text, shape in text_boxes.items():
    print(f"{text} text: left={shape.left.inches:.2f}, top={shape.top.inches:.2f}")

# Map: left picture -> shark, center picture -> car, right picture -> baseball
mapping = {
    'left': 'shark',
    'center': 'car', 
    'right': 'baseball'
}

# Choose a uniform size (use the middle size or a reasonable size)
uniform_width = Inches(3.0)
uniform_height = Inches(3.0)

# Choose a uniform top position for all pictures (below title, above text)
uniform_top = Inches(2.0)

print(f"\nApplying uniform size: {uniform_width.inches:.2f} x {uniform_height.inches:.2f}")
print(f"Applying uniform top: {uniform_top.inches:.2f}")

for pic_pos, text_label in mapping.items():
    pic = pictures[pic_pos]
    txt = text_boxes[text_label]
    
    # Set uniform size
    pic.width = uniform_width
    pic.height = uniform_height
    
    # Set uniform top (horizontal alignment)
    pic.top = uniform_top
    
    # Center horizontally above text
    txt_center = txt.left + txt.width / 2
    new_left = txt_center - pic.width / 2
    pic.left = new_left
    
    print(f"{text_label}: pic centered at {new_left.inches:.2f}, top={uniform_top.inches:.2f}")

# Save
prs.save(path)
print(f"\nSaved to: {path}")
```

```python
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt
from pptx.dml.color import RGBColor
from pptx.enum.shapes import MSO_SHAPE
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN, MSO_ANCHOR
import os

# Load presentation
file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_81_add_company_logo_pnqwky4p/case_81_add_company_logo_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(file_path)

# Logo settings - top right corner
margin_right = Inches(0.3)
margin_top = Inches(0.3)
logo_width = Inches(1.2)
logo_height = Inches(0.5)

slide_width = prs.slide_width
logo_left = slide_width - logo_width - margin_right
logo_top = margin_top

print(f"Adding logo to {len(prs.slides)} slides at position ({logo_left.inches:.2f}, {logo_top.inches:.2f})")

# Add logo to each slide
for i, slide in enumerate(prs.slides):
    shape = slide.shapes.add_shape(
        MSO_SHAPE.ROUNDED_RECTANGLE,
        logo_left,
        logo_top,
        logo_width,
        logo_height
    )
    
    shape.fill.solid()
    shape.fill.fore_color.rgb = RGBColor(0, 102, 204)
    shape.line.color.rgb = RGBColor(0, 51, 102)
    shape.line.width = Pt(1.5)
    
    text_frame = shape.text_frame
    text_frame.word_wrap = False
    p = text_frame.paragraphs[0]
    p.text = "LOGO"
    p.font.size = Pt(18)
    p.font.bold = True
    p.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(255, 255, 255)
    p.alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER
    text_frame.vertical_anchor = MSO_ANCHOR.MIDDLE
    
    print(f"  Added to slide {i+1}")

# Save
prs.save(file_path)
print(f"Saved to {file_path}")
```

```python
from pptx import Presentation
```
```python
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN
import qrcode
from io import BytesIO

# Load the presentation
file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_91_add_qr_code_94bnxpbf/case_91_add_qr_code_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(file_path)

# Get the last slide
last_slide = prs.slides[-1]
print(f"Last slide has {len(last_slide.shapes)} shapes")

# Generate QR code
url = "https://github.com/michaelofengend/PPTPilot"
qr = qrcode.QRCode(
    version=1,
    error_correction=qrcode.constants.ERROR_CORRECT_L,
    box_size=10,
    border=4,
)
qr.add_data(url)
qr.make(fit=True)

# Create QR code image
qr_img = qr.make_image(fill_color="black", back_color="white")

# Save to bytes buffer
img_buffer = BytesIO()
qr_img.save(img_buffer, format='PNG')
img_buffer.seek(0)

# Position the QR code in the center-bottom area of the slide
slide_width = prs.slide_width
slide_height = prs.slide_height

# QR code size: 2 inches
qr_size = Inches(2.0)

# Center horizontally, place in lower portion
qr_left = (slide_width - qr_size) / 2
qr_top = Inches(4.5)

# Add QR code image
qr_picture = last_slide.shapes.add_picture(img_buffer, qr_left, qr_top, width=qr_size)

# Add text box below QR code
caption_text = "GitHub: michaelofengend/PPTPilot"
text_box_width = Inches(4.0)
text_box_height = Inches(0.5)

# Center the text box below the QR code
text_left = (slide_width - text_box_width) / 2
text_top = qr_top + qr_size + Inches(0.2)

text_box = last_slide.shapes.add_textbox(text_left, text_top, text_box_width, text_box_height)
text_frame = text_box.text_frame
text_frame.text = caption_text

# Center align the text
paragraph = text_frame.paragraphs[0]
paragraph.alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER
paragraph.font.size = Pt(14)
paragraph.font.bold = True

# Save the presentation
prs.save(file_path)
print(f"Saved to {file_path}")
print(f"Added QR code and caption text")
```

```python
            if shape.fill.type == MSO_FILL_TYPE.SOLID:
                try:
                    print(f"       Fill color: {shape.fill.fore_color.rgb}")
                except:
                    pass
            print(f"       Line color: {shape.line.color.rgb if hasattr(shape.line.color, 'rgb') else 'N/A'}")
    print()
```

```python
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt
from pptx.dml.color import RGBColor
from pptx.enum.shapes import MSO_SHAPE

# Load the presentation
path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_84_add_progress_bar_i0pwhwlb/case_84_add_progress_bar_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(path)

# Progress bar settings
bar_left = Inches(1.0)  # 1 inch margin from left
bar_width = Inches(18.0)  # 18 inches wide (20 - 2 inch margins)
bar_height = Inches(0.3)  # 0.3 inches tall
bar_top = Inches(10.7)  # Near bottom of slide (11.25 - 0.3 - 0.25 margin)

# Colors
unfilled_color = RGBColor(200, 200, 200)  # Light gray
filled_color = RGBColor(0, 112, 192)  # Blue

num_slides = len(prs.slides)

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides):
    # Calculate progress (1-based index)
    progress = (slide_idx + 1) / num_slides
    
    # Add background bar (unfilled portion)
    bg_bar = slide.shapes.add_shape(
        MSO_SHAPE.RECTANGLE,
        bar_left,
        bar_top,
        bar_width,
        bar_height
    )
    bg_bar.fill.solid()
    bg_bar.fill.fore_color.rgb = unfilled_color
    bg_bar.line.fill.background()  # No border
    
    # Add filled portion
    filled_width = Inches(18.0 * progress)
    filled_bar = slide.shapes.add_shape(
        MSO_SHAPE.RECTANGLE,
        bar_left,
        bar_top,
        filled_width,
        bar_height
    )
    filled_bar.fill.solid()
    filled_bar.fill.fore_color.rgb = filled_color
    filled_bar.line.fill.background()  # No border
    
    print(f"Slide {slide_idx + 1}: Added progress bar with {progress*100:.1f}% filled")

# Save the presentation
prs.save(path)
print(f"\nSaved presentation to: {path}")
```

```python
from lxml import etree

        print(f"Slide {i+1}: transition type = {trans_type}")
    else:
```

```python
MARGIN_RIGHT = Inches(0.5)

CONTENT_WIDTH = slide_width - MARGIN_LEFT - MARGIN_RIGHT - LANE_LABEL_WIDTH

# Standard box size
BOX_WIDTH = Inches(2.0)
BOX_HEIGHT = Inches(0.8)
BADGE_SIZE = Inches(0.35)

# Colors
LANE_COLORS = [
    RGBColor(230, 240, 255),  # Light blue for Intake
    RGBColor(255, 240, 230),  # Light orange for Processing
    RGBColor(230, 255, 230),  # Light green for Output
]

# Title
title_box = slide.shapes.add_textbox(MARGIN_LEFT, MARGIN_TOP, slide_width - MARGIN_LEFT - MARGIN_RIGHT, Inches(0.6))

]
```

```python
# Function to add a process box with badge
def add_process_box(slide, x, y, width, height, text, badge_num, fill_color=None):
    # Ensure integer positions
    x = int(x)
    y = int(y)
    width = int(width)
    height = int(height)
    
    # Main box
    box = slide.shapes.add_shape(MSO_SHAPE.ROUNDED_RECTANGLE, x, y, width, height)
    if fill_color:
        box.fill.solid()
        box.fill.fore_color.rgb = fill_color
    else:
        box.fill.solid()
        box.fill.fore_color.rgb = RGBColor(240, 240, 240)
    
    # Add text
    tf = box.text_frame
    tf.word_wrap = True
    p = tf.paragraphs[0]
    p.text = text
    p.font.size = Pt(12)
    p.alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER
    tf.paragraphs[0].font.bold = True
    
    # Badge (small circle with number)
    badge_x = int(x - BADGE_SIZE / 2)
    badge_y = int(y - BADGE_SIZE / 2)
    badge = slide.shapes.add_shape(MSO_SHAPE.OVAL, badge_x, badge_y, int(BADGE_SIZE), int(BADGE_SIZE))
    badge.fill.solid()
    badge.fill.fore_color.rgb = RGBColor(0, 112, 192)
    badge.line.color.rgb = RGBColor(255, 255, 255)
    badge.line.width = Pt(2)
    
    tf_badge = badge.text_frame
    tf_badge.paragraphs[0].text = str(badge_num)
    tf_badge.paragraphs[0].font.size = Pt(14)
    tf_badge.paragraphs[0].font.bold = True
    tf_badge.paragraphs[0].font.color.rgb = RGBColor(255, 255, 255)
    tf_badge.paragraphs[0].alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER
    
    return box, badge

# Function to add diamond shape
def add_diamond(slide, x, y, width, height, text):
    x, y, width, height = int(x), int(y), int(width), int(height)
    diamond = slide.shapes.add_shape(MSO_SHAPE.DIAMOND, x, y, width, height)
    diamond.fill.solid()
    diamond.fill.fore_color.rgb = RGBColor(255, 230, 200)
    
    tf = diamond.text_frame
    p = tf.paragraphs[0]
    p.text = text
    p.font.size = Pt(14)
    p.font.bold = True
    p.alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER
    
    return diamond
```

```python
from pptx import Presentation
import re

# Load the presentation
path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_51_currency_symbol_swap_eurusd_2t8_u1_w/case_51_currency_symbol_swap_eurusd_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(path)

def convert_euro_to_usd(text):
    """
    Convert Euro format (e.g., "40.000 €", "3.494.724  €") to USD format (e.g., "$40,000", "$3,494,724")
    Pattern: number with dots as thousands separator, optional spaces, then € symbol
    """
    # Pattern to match Euro amounts: digits with dots as thousands separators, optional spaces, then €
    # Examples: "40.000 €", "3.494.724  €", "100.000€"
    pattern = r'(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{3})+(?:\.\d{3})*)\s*€'
    
    def replace_match(match):
        euro_amount = match.group(1)
        # Replace dots with commas for USD format
        usd_amount = euro_amount.replace('.', ',')
        return f'${usd_amount}'
    
    return re.sub(pattern, replace_match, text)

# Process slides 2 and 3 (indices 1 and 2)
for i, slide in enumerate(prs.slides):
    if i in [1, 2]:  # slides 2 and 3
        print(f"\n=== Processing Slide {i+1} ===")
        for shape in slide.shapes:
            # Process text in text frames
            if shape.has_text_frame:
                for para in shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
                    for run in para.runs:
                        original_text = run.text
                        converted_text = convert_euro_to_usd(original_text)
                        if original_text != converted_text:
                            print(f"  Converting: '{original_text}' -> '{converted_text}'")
                            run.text = converted_text
            
            # Process tables
            if shape.has_table:
                table = shape.table
                for row in table.rows:
                    for cell in row.cells:
                        # Process each paragraph in the cell
                        for para in cell.text_frame.paragraphs:
                            for run in para.runs:
                                original_text = run.text
                                converted_text = convert_euro_to_usd(original_text)
                                if original_text != converted_text:
                                    print(f"  Table cell converting: '{original_text}' -> '{converted_text}'")
                                    run.text = converted_text

# Save the modified presentation
prs.save(path)
print(f"\nSaved to: {path}")
```

```python
from pptx import Presentation

# Load the presentation
file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_pptarena_case_61_sort_by_score_and_crop_image_169_p3xg6zjx/case_61_sort_by_score_and_crop_image_169_src.pptx"
prs = Presentation(file_path)

# Get the table from the first slide
slide = prs.slides[0]
table = None
for shape in slide.shapes:
    if shape.has_table:
        table = shape.table
        break

# Extract data rows (skip header)
data_rows = []
for row_idx in range(1, len(table.rows)):
    name = table.rows[row_idx].cells[0].text
    team = table.rows[row_idx].cells[1].text
    score = int(table.rows[row_idx].cells[2].text)
    data_rows.append((name, team, score))

print("Original data rows:")
for row in data_rows:
    print(f"  {row}")

# Sort by score in descending order
data_rows.sort(key=lambda x: x[2], reverse=True)

print("\nSorted data rows:")
for row in data_rows:
    print(f"  {row}")

# Update the table with sorted data
for row_idx, (name, team, score) in enumerate(data_rows, start=1):
    table.rows[row_idx].cells[0].text = name
    table.rows[row_idx].cells[1].text = team
    table.rows[row_idx].cells[2].text = str(score)

# Verify the update
print("\nUpdated table:")
for row_idx, row in enumerate(table.rows):
    row_data = [cell.text for cell in row.cells]
    print(f"  Row {row_idx}: {row_data}")

# Save the presentation
prs.save(file_path)
print(f"\nSaved to: {file_path}")
```

```python
            for para in shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
                for run in para.runs:
                    run.font.color.rgb = LIGHT_TEXT

        if hasattr(shape, 'table') and shape.has_table:
            table = shape.table
            for row in table.rows:
                for cell in row.cells:

                        for para in cell.text_frame.paragraphs:

                                run.font.color.rgb = LIGHT_TEXT

# Update slide master

master_fill = master.background.fill
master_fill.solid()
master_fill.fore_color.rgb = DARK_BG
```

```python
# Define On-Peak hours (PJM: Monday-Friday, 7 AM to 10 PM)
def is_on_peak(date, hour):
    if pd.isna(hour):
        return False
    hour_int = int(hour)
    if date.weekday() < 5:  # Monday to Friday
        if 7 <= hour_int <= 22:
            return True
    return False

df['Is_OnPeak'] = df.apply(lambda row: is_on_peak(row['Date'], row['Hour']), axis=1)

# Calculate daily aggregates
zones = ['RT_WESTERNHUB', 'RT_EASTERNHUB', 'RT_DPLSOUTH', 'RT_NYPPE', 'RT_WESCOSVI',
         'DA_WESTERNHUB', 'DA_EASTERNHUB', 'DA_DPLSOUTH', 'DA_NYPDE', 'DA_WESCOSVI']

daily_stats = []
for date, group in df.groupby('Date'):
    stats = {'Date': date}
    for zone in zones:
        if zone in df.columns:
            atc_vals = group[zone].dropna()
            stats[f'{zone}_ATC'] = atc_vals.mean() if len(atc_vals) > 0 else None
            
            onpeak_vals = group[group['Is_OnPeak']][zone].dropna()
            stats[f'{zone}_OnPeak'] = onpeak_vals.mean() if len(onpeak_vals) > 0 else None
            
            offpeak_vals = group[~group['Is_OnPeak']][zone].dropna()
            stats[f'{zone}_OffPeak'] = offpeak_vals.mean() if len(offpeak_vals) > 0 else None
    daily_stats.append(stats)

daily_df = pd.DataFrame(daily_stats)
daily_df['Date_Only'] = daily_df['Date'].dt.date
daily_lookup = {row['Date_Only']: row for _, row in daily_df.iterrows()}

print(f"Daily stats calculated: {len(daily_df)} days")

# Map zones to Strips columns (17 zones, 3 cols each: On-Peak, Off-Peak, ATC)
zone_mapping = {}
zone_idx = 1
for z in zones:
    zone_mapping[zone_idx] = (z, 'RT' if z.startswith('RT_') else 'DA')
    zone_idx += 1

print(f"Zone mapping: {len(zone_mapping)} zones")

# Populate Strips sheet
strips_dates = []
for i in range(7, strips.max_row + 1):
    val = strips.cell(row=i, column=1).value
    if isinstance(val, datetime):
        strips_dates.append((i, val))

populated = 0
for row_idx, strips_date in strips_dates:
    date_key = strips_date.date()
    if date_key in daily_lookup:
        row_data = daily_lookup[date_key]
        for zone_idx, (zone_name, _) in zone_mapping.items():
            base_col = 2 + (zone_idx - 1) * 3
            for j, suffix in enumerate(['_OnPeak', '_OffPeak', '_ATC']):
                val = row_data.get(f'{zone_name}{suffix}')
                if pd.notna(val):
                    strips.cell(row=row_idx, column=base_col + j).value = round(float(val), 4)
        populated += 1

print(f"Populated {populated} rows in Strips sheet")
```

```python
df['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Date'])
df['Hour'] = pd.to_numeric(df['Hour'], errors='coerce') / 100

# Define On-Peak hours (PJM: Monday-Friday, 7 AM to 10 PM)
def is_on_peak(date, hour):
    if pd.isna(hour):
        return False
    hour_int = int(hour)
    if date.weekday() < 5:
        if 7 <= hour_int <= 22:
            return True
    return False

df['Is_OnPeak'] = df.apply(lambda row: is_on_peak(row['Date'], row['Hour']), axis=1)

# Calculate daily aggregates
zones = ['RT_WESTERNHUB', 'RT_EASTERNHUB', 'RT_DPLSOUTH', 'RT_NYPPE', 'RT_WESCOSVI',
         'DA_WESTERNHUB', 'DA_EASTERNHUB', 'DA_DPLSOUTH', 'DA_NYPDE', 'DA_WESCOSVI']

daily_stats = []
for date, group in df.groupby('Date'):
    stats = {'Date': date}
    for zone in zones:
        atc_vals = group[zone].dropna()
        stats[f'{zone}_ATC'] = atc_vals.mean() if len(atc_vals) > 0 else None
        
        onpeak_vals = group[group['Is_OnPeak']][zone].dropna()
        stats[f'{zone}_OnPeak'] = onpeak_vals.mean() if len(onpeak_vals) > 0 else None
        
        offpeak_vals = group[~group['Is_OnPeak']][zone].dropna()
        stats[f'{zone}_OffPeak'] = offpeak_vals.mean() if len(offpeak_vals) > 0 else None
    daily_stats.append(stats)

daily_df = pd.DataFrame(daily_stats)
daily_df['Date_Only'] = daily_df['Date'].dt.date
daily_lookup = {row['Date_Only']: row for _, row in daily_df.iterrows()}

# Map zones to Strips columns
zone_mapping = {i+1: zone for i, zone in enumerate(zones)}

# Populate Strips sheet

for i in range(7, strips.max_row + 1):
    val = strips.cell(row=i, column=1).value
    if isinstance(val, datetime):
        strips_dates.append((i, val))

populated = 0

    if date_key in daily_lookup:
        row_data = daily_lookup[date_key]
        for zone_idx, zone_name in zone_mapping.items():

                val = row_data.get(f'{zone_name}{suffix}')
                if pd.notna(val):
                    strips.cell(row=row_idx, column=base_col + j).value = round(float(val), 4)
        populated += 1

print(f"Populated {populated} rows in Strips sheet")

            vals = group[col].dropna()

                stats[f'{col}_Avg'] = vals.mean()
                stats[f'{col}_Max'] = vals.max()
                stats[f'{col}_Min'] = vals.min()
    monthly_stats.append(stats)

monthly_df = pd.DataFrame(monthly_stats)
```

```python
from openpyxl import load_workbook
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_finch_170_cawf9lv4/170_src_0.xlsx"
wb = load_workbook(file_path)
strips = wb['Strips']
raw_data = wb['Raw Data']

# Load Raw Data
data = []
for i in range(3, raw_data.max_row + 1):
    row_data = [raw_data.cell(row=i, column=j).value for j in range(1, 13)]
    if row_data[0]:
        data.append(row_data)

df = pd.DataFrame(data, columns=['Date', 'Hour', 'RT_WESTERNHUB', 'RT_EASTERNHUB', 
                                  'RT_DPLSOUTH', 'RT_NYPPE', 'RT_WESCOSVI',
                                  'DA_WESTERNHUB', 'DA_EASTERNHUB', 'DA_DPLSOUTH', 
                                  'DA_NYPDE', 'DA_WESCOSVI'])

df['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Date'])
df['Hour'] = pd.to_numeric(df['Hour'], errors='coerce') / 100

# On-Peak: Monday-Friday, 7 AM to 10 PM
def is_on_peak(date, hour):
    if pd.isna(hour):
        return False
    hour_int = int(hour)
    if date.weekday() < 5:
        if 7 <= hour_int <= 22:
            return True
    return False

df['Is_OnPeak'] = df.apply(lambda row: is_on_peak(row['Date'], row['Hour']), axis=1)

# Calculate daily aggregates
zones = ['RT_WESTERNHUB', 'RT_EASTERNHUB', 'RT_DPLSOUTH', 'RT_NYPPE', 'RT_WESCOSVI',
         'DA_WESTERNHUB', 'DA_EASTERNHUB', 'DA_DPLSOUTH', 'DA_NYPDE', 'DA_WESCOSVI']

daily_stats = []
for date, group in df.groupby('Date'):
    stats = {'Date': date}
    for zone in zones:
        atc_vals = group[zone].dropna()
        stats[f'{zone}_ATC'] = atc_vals.mean() if len(atc_vals) > 0 else None
        
        onpeak_vals = group[group['Is_OnPeak']][zone].dropna()
        stats[f'{zone}_OnPeak'] = onpeak_vals.mean() if len(onpeak_vals) > 0 else None
        
        offpeak_vals = group[~group['Is_OnPeak']][zone].dropna()
        stats[f'{zone}_OffPeak'] = offpeak_vals.mean() if len(offpeak_vals) > 0 else None
    daily_stats.append(stats)

daily_df = pd.DataFrame(daily_stats)
daily_df['Date_Only'] = daily_df['Date'].dt.date
daily_lookup = {row['Date_Only']: row for _, row in daily_df.iterrows()}

print(f"Daily lookup has {len(daily_lookup)} dates")
print(f"Available range: {min(daily_lookup.keys())} to {max(daily_lookup.keys())}")

# Get all Strips dates (including formula-calculated ones)
start_date = datetime(2002, 1, 1)
all_strips_dates = []

# Rows 8-73: Formula dates (2002-01-01 to 2002-03-07)
for i in range(8, 74):
    day_offset = i - 8
    date_val = start_date + timedelta(days=day_offset)
    all_strips_dates.append((i, date_val))

# Rows 74+: Hardcoded dates
for i in range(74, strips.max_row + 1):
    val = strips.cell(row=i, column=1).value
    if isinstance(val, datetime):
        all_strips_dates.append((i, val))

print(f"Total Strips dates: {len(all_strips_dates)}")

# Populate Strips sheet
zone_mapping = {i+1: zone for i, zone in enumerate(zones)}
populated = 0
matched = 0

for row_idx, strips_date in all_strips_dates:
    date_key = strips_date.date()
    if date_key in daily_lookup:
        matched += 1
        row_data = daily_lookup[date_key]
        for zone_idx, zone_name in zone_mapping.items():
            base_col = 2 + (zone_idx - 1) * 3
            for j, suffix in enumerate(['_OnPeak', '_OffPeak', '_ATC']):
                val = row_data.get(f'{zone_name}{suffix}')
                if pd.notna(val):
                    strips.cell(row=row_idx, column=base_col + j).value = round(float(val), 4)
                    populated += 1

print(f"Matched {matched} dates")
print(f"Populated {populated} cells")
wb.save(file_path)
print("Saved!")
```

```python
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the workbook
file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_finch_47_nsfoskb5/47_src_0.xlsx"
wb = load_workbook(file_path)
ws_income = wb['Income Statement']

# First, let's check the current values in the Income Statement
print("Current Income Statement values:")
print(f"Row 12 (Operating Income) I12: {ws_income['I12'].value}")
print(f"Row 14 (Other income or loss) I14: {ws_income['I14'].value}")
print(f"Row 16 (Interest expenses) I16: {ws_income['I16'].value}")
print(f"Row 31 (Common shares outstanding) I31: {ws_income['I31'].value}")

# Now let's calculate and fill in the missing values
# From Balance Sheet:
# - Goodwill (Purchase Method): $5,600 million
# - Increase in asset value due to revaluation: $90 million

# Amortization calculations:
# - Goodwill amortized over 40 years: 5600 / 40 = 140
# - Extra depreciation amortized over 10 years: 90 / 10 = 9

goodwill = 5600
extra_depreciation_base = 90
goodwill_amortization = goodwill / 40  # 140
extra_depreciation_amortization = extra_depreciation_base / 10  # 9

print(f"\nCalculated values:")
print(f"Amortization of goodwill: {goodwill_amortization}")
print(f"Amortization of extra depreciation: {extra_depreciation_amortization}")

# Update the Income Statement
# Row 18: Amortization of goodwill (Purchase Method column I)
ws_income['I18'] = -goodwill_amortization  # Negative because it's an expense

# Row 23: Amortization of extra depreciation (Purchase Method column I)
ws_income['I23'] = -extra_depreciation_amortization  # Negative because it's an expense

# Row 25: Income before income taxes
# Formula: Operating Income + Other income or loss - Interest expenses - Amortization of goodwill - Amortization of extra depreciation
# I12 (Operating Income) + I14 (Other income) + I16 (Interest expenses) + I18 (Goodwill amort) + I23 (Extra dep amort)
ws_income['I25'] = '=I12+I14+I16+I18+I23'

# Row 27: Income tax (35.3% for Purchase method)
ws_income['I27'] = '=-I25*0.353'

# Row 29: Net income
ws_income['I29'] = '=I25+I27'

# Row 32: Earnings per share ($/share)
# Net income / Common shares outstanding
ws_income['I32'] = '=I29/I31*1000'  # Multiply by 1000 because income is in millions and shares are in millions

# Save the workbook
wb.save(file_path)
print(f"\nWorkbook saved to {file_path}")

# Verify the changes
print("\nUpdated Income Statement (Purchase Method column):")
for row_num in [18, 23, 25, 27, 29, 32]:
    cell_val = ws_income[f'I{row_num}'].value
    print(f"Row {row_num}: {cell_val}")
```

```python
print(f"Sales: {sales}")

print(f"Extra Depreciation Amortization: {extra_dep_amort}")
print(f"Income Before Tax: {income_before_tax}")
print(f"Income Tax (35.3%): {income_tax}")
print(f"Net Income: {net_income}")
print(f"EPS: {eps}")
```

```python
# Count names in each group
def count_names_in_group(ws, start_row, end_row):
    count = 0
    for row in range(start_row, end_row + 1):
        for col in range(2, 15):  # Columns B to N (departments)
            val = ws.cell(row=row, column=col).value
            if val is not None and isinstance(val, str) and val.strip():
                count += 1
    return count

# Group 1: rows 7-12

# Group 2: rows 15-21

# Group 3: rows 24-28

total = group1_count + group2_count + group3_count

print(f"Group 1 count: {group1_count}")
print(f"Group 2 count: {group2_count}")
print(f"Group 3 count: {group3_count}")
print(f"Total: {total}")

pct1 = round(group1_count / total * 100, 2) if total > 0 else 0
pct2 = round(group2_count / total * 100, 2) if total > 0 else 0
pct3 = round(group3_count / total * 100, 2) if total > 0 else 0

print(f"\nGroup 1: {group1_count} ({pct1}%)")
print(f"Group 2: {group2_count} ({pct2}%)")

ws2['B1'] = 'Headcount'
ws2['C1'] = 'Percentage'
```

```python
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the workbook
file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_finch_78_l5wdtg1v/78_src_0.xlsx"
wb = load_workbook(file_path)
ets_sheet = wb['ETS']

sheets_map = {'NNG': 2, 'TW': 3, 'Citrus': 4, 'NB': 5, 'GCO_HPL': 6, 'Other': 7}

# Check for all discrepancies more carefully
print("--- Finding all discrepancies ---")
discrepancies = []

for i in range(1, ets_sheet.max_row + 1):
    ets_row = list(ets_sheet.iter_rows(min_row=i, max_row=i, values_only=False))[0]
    for sheet_name, col_idx in sheets_map.items():
        sheet = wb[sheet_name]
        sheet_row = list(sheet.iter_rows(min_row=i, max_row=i, values_only=False))[0]
        
        ets_cell = ets_row[col_idx]
        sheet_cell = sheet_row[2]
        
        ets_val = ets_cell.value
        sheet_val = sheet_cell.value
        
        # Skip if both are None
        if ets_val is None and sheet_val is None:
            continue
            
        # Skip if both are the same
        if ets_val == sheet_val:
            continue
            
        # Skip if one is 0 and other is None
        if (ets_val is None and sheet_val == 0) or (ets_val == 0 and sheet_val is None):
            continue
            
        # Check if ETS has a calculated value but sheet has formula
        if isinstance(sheet_val, str) and sheet_val.startswith('='):
            if ets_val != sheet_val:
                discrepancies.append((i, sheet_name, col_idx, ets_val, sheet_val))
                print(f"Row {i}, {sheet_name}: ETS={ets_val!r}, Sheet={sheet_val!r}")
        # Check if values are different numbers
        elif isinstance(ets_val, (int, float)) and isinstance(sheet_val, (int, float)):
            if ets_val != sheet_val:
                discrepancies.append((i, sheet_name, col_idx, ets_val, sheet_val))
                print(f"Row {i}, {sheet_name}: ETS={ets_val!r}, Sheet={sheet_val!r}")

print(f"\nTotal discrepancies found: {len(discrepancies)}")

# Now fix the discrepancies by updating ETS to match the sub-sheets
print("\n--- Fixing discrepancies ---")
for row_num, sheet_name, col_idx, ets_val, sheet_val in discrepancies:
    cell = ets_sheet.cell(row=row_num, column=col_idx+1)  # openpyxl uses 1-based indexing
    print(f"Updating ETS row {row_num}, col {col_idx+1} from {ets_val!r} to {sheet_val!r}")
    cell.value = sheet_val

# Save the workbook
wb.save(file_path)
print(f"\nSaved workbook to {file_path}")
```

```python
wb = load_workbook(file_path)

ws = wb['Summary']

# Blue cells in column Q that need to be calculated as I + K + L + N + O
q_cells = ['Q13', 'Q24', 'Q33', 'Q41', 'Q47']

print("=== Calculating Q values (I + K + L + N + O) ===")
for coord in q_cells:
    row = int(coord[1:])  # Extract row number
    i_val = ws.cell(row=row, column=9).value  # Column I
    k_val = ws.cell(row=row, column=11).value  # Column K
    l_val = ws.cell(row=row, column=12).value  # Column L
    n_val = ws.cell(row=row, column=14).value  # Column N
    o_val = ws.cell(row=row, column=15).value  # Column O
    
    print(f"\n{coord} (row {row}):")
    print(f"  I{row}: {i_val}")
    print(f"  K{row}: {k_val}")
    print(f"  L{row}: {l_val}")
    print(f"  N{row}: {n_val}")
    print(f"  O{row}: {o_val}")
    
    # Calculate sum
    values = [v for v in [i_val, k_val, l_val, n_val, o_val] if v is not None and isinstance(v, (int, float))]
    if values:
        total = sum(values)
        print(f"  Sum: {total}")

# Now look at row 72 blue cells (E72, F72, H72, I72)
print("\n\n=== Row 72 - Ending Balance ===")
row = 72
for col in ['E', 'F', 'H', 'I']:
    col_num = ord(col) - ord('A') + 1
    cell = ws.cell(row=row, column=col_num)
    print(f"{col}{row}: {cell.value}")

# Look at the data above row 72 to understand what should be summed
print("\n=== Data above row 72 ===")
for r in range(65, 72):
    print(f"\nRow {r}:")
    for c in ['E', 'F', 'H', 'I']:
        col_num = ord(c) - ord('A') + 1
        val = ws.cell(row=r, column=col_num).value
        if val is not None:
            print(f"  {c}{r}: {val}")
```

```python
import openpyxl

# Load the workbook
file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_finch_86_t7kl_a6_/86_src_0.xlsx"
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(file_path)
ws1 = wb['Sheet1']

# Read current data
print("Reading current data:")
for i in range(1, 15):
    label = ws1.cell(row=i, column=1).value
    value = ws1.cell(row=i, column=2).value
    print(f"Row {i}: {label} | {value}")

# Extract values
cash = ws1['B1'].value
print(f"\nCash: {cash}")

# Equity items are in rows 2-8, column B
equity_items = []
for row in range(2, 9):
    val = ws1.cell(row=row, column=2).value
    if val is not None:
        equity_items.append(val)
print(f"Equity items: {equity_items}")

# Calculate totals
total_equity = sum(equity_items)
print(f"Total Equity: {total_equity}")

# Check if there's a Fixed Income value
fixed_income = ws1['B10'].value
if fixed_income is None:
    fixed_income = 0
print(f"Fixed Income: {fixed_income}")

total_assets = cash + total_equity + fixed_income
print(f"Total Assets: {total_assets}")

# Calculate percentages
cash_pct = round((cash / total_assets) * 100, 2)
equity_pct = round((total_equity / total_assets) * 100, 2)
fixed_income_pct = round((fixed_income / total_assets) * 100, 2)

print(f"\nCash %: {cash_pct}")
print(f"Equity %: {equity_pct}")
print(f"Fixed Income %: {fixed_income_pct}")
print(f"Sum of percentages: {cash_pct + equity_pct + fixed_income_pct}")

# Fill in the blanks
ws1['B9'] = total_equity   # Total Equity
ws1['B10'] = fixed_income  # Fixed Income
ws1['B11'] = total_assets  # Total
ws1['B12'] = cash_pct      # Cash %
ws1['B13'] = equity_pct    # Equity %
ws1['B14'] = fixed_income_pct  # Fixed Income %

# Save the workbook
wb.save(file_path)
print(f"\nWorkbook saved to {file_path}")
```

```python
import openpyxl
from openpyxl.styles import Font, Border, Side
from openpyxl.utils import get_column_letter

# Load the workbook
file_path = '/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_finch_76_nwntsp_v/76_src_0.xlsx'
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(file_path)

# Get Sheet1
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Define styles
bold_font = Font(bold=True)
thin_border = Border(
    bottom=Side(style='thin')
)

# 1. Bold the titles (row 4 - header row)
for col in range(1, ws.max_column + 1):
    cell = ws.cell(row=4, column=col)
    cell.font = bold_font

# 2. Identify department rows and add borders between them
# Looking at the data, departments are:
# Row 5-6: Canada
# Row 7: Operations
# Row 8: Gas Commercial
# Row 9: Power Commercial
# Row 10: Finance
# Row 11: Treasury
# Row 12-13: IT Development
# Row 14: IT Infrastructure
# Row 15-16: Credit
# Row 17: Legal
# Row 18: Regulatory
# Row 19-20: Risk
# Row 21: HR

# Add borders after each department section
# Border after row 6 (end of Canada)
# Border after row 7 (end of Operations)
# Border after row 8 (end of Gas Commercial)
# Border after row 9 (end of Power Commercial)
# Border after row 10 (end of Finance)
# Border after row 11 (end of Treasury)
# Border after row 13 (end of IT Development)
# Border after row 14 (end of IT Infrastructure)
# Border after row 16 (end of Credit)
# Border after row 17 (end of Legal)
# Border after row 18 (end of Regulatory)
# Border after row 20 (end of Risk)
# Border after row 21 (end of HR)

border_rows = [6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 16, 17, 18, 20, 21]

for row in border_rows:
    for col in range(1, ws.max_column + 1):
        cell = ws.cell(row=row, column=col)
        cell.border = thin_border

# Save the workbook
wb.save(file_path)
print(f"Workbook saved to {file_path}")
print("Formatting applied:")
print("- Row 4 (titles) is now bold")
print(f"- Borders added after rows: {border_rows}")

```

```python
        departments.append((i+1, val))  # Store column index and department name

# Find group boundaries
groups = {}
current_group = None
for row_num, row in all_data:
    if row[0] in (1, 2, 3):
        current_group = row[0]
        groups[current_group] = {'start': row_num, 'end': None}
    elif current_group and row[0] is None:
        # Check if this is still part of current group or next group started
        pass

# Find end of each group
group_starts = sorted([v['start'] for v in groups.values()])
for i, start in enumerate(group_starts):
    if i < len(group_starts) - 1:
        groups[list(groups.keys())[i]]['end'] = group_starts[i+1] - 1
    else:
        groups[list(groups.keys())[i]]['end'] = 188
```

```python
        for col_idx, dept_name in departments:
            if row[col_idx - 1]:  # col_idx is 1-based, so subtract 1 for 0-based index
                summary[group_num][dept_name] += 1

print("Summary by Group and Department:")
for group in sorted(summary.keys()):
    print(f"\nGroup {group}:")
    for dept, count in sorted(summary[group].items()):
```

```python
# Create a stacked area chart
from openpyxl.chart import AreaChart, Reference
from openpyxl.chart.series import DataPoint

# Create the chart
chart = AreaChart()
chart.type = "area"  # Stacked area chart
chart.style = 10
chart.title = "Existing and Proposed Deals with Huber"
chart.y_axis.title = "Volume (MMBtu/d)"
chart.x_axis.title = "Date"
chart.grouping = "stacked"  # This makes it a stacked area chart

# Data for the chart
# Dates are in column A, rows 4-64
# Values are in columns B-H, rows 4-64
# Headers are in row 3, columns B-H

# Add data series - each column B through H is a different series
data = Reference(ws_volumes, min_col=2, min_row=3, max_col=8, max_row=64)
cats = Reference(ws_volumes, min_col=1, min_row=4, max_row=64)  # Dates

chart.add_data(data, titles_from_data=True)
chart.set_categories(cats)

# Set chart size
chart.width = 20
chart.height = 12

# Add the chart to the Summary sheet
ws_summary.add_chart(chart, "A25")

# Save the workbook
wb.save(file_path)
print(f"Chart added to Summary sheet at cell A25")
print(f"Chart title: {chart.title}")
print(f"Data range: Volumes!B3:H64")
print(f"Categories: Volumes!A4:A64")
print(f"File saved to: {file_path}")
```

```python
import openpyxl
```

```python
from openpyxl.chart.label import DataLabelList
```

```python
import openpyxl

# Load the workbook
path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_task_1_b20lso4a/119_src_0.xlsx"
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(path)
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Find unique plants - filter out non-plant entries
non_plant_entries = {'NEW', '(Hays Road Plant)', 'EXISTING UNITS', 'NEW UNITS', 'EXISTING', 
                     'Phase 2', 'Utility Peaker', '(This is a 4 & 2 plant', 
                     'Units 1-4 combustion', 'Units 5-6 steam', 'Peaking units'}

plants = set()
plant_list = []

for row in ws.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=ws.max_row, values_only=True):
    plant_name = row[1]  # Column B
    if plant_name and isinstance(plant_name, str):
        plant_clean = plant_name.strip()
        # Skip header and non-plant entries
        if plant_clean not in non_plant_entries and plant_clean != 'Plant':
            # Also check if it has a company in column A or Total MW in column C
            company = row[0]
            total_mw = row[2]
            # Only count if it looks like a plant entry (has company or Total MW)
            if company or total_mw:
                if plant_clean not in plants:
                    plants.add(plant_clean)
                    plant_list.append(plant_clean)

print(f"Number of unique plants: {len(plants)}")
print("\nAll unique plants:")
for i, plant in enumerate(sorted(plant_list), 1):
    print(f"{i}. {plant}")

# Let's also verify by looking at rows with both company and plant
print("\n--- Verification: rows with Company and Plant ---")
count = 0
for i, row in enumerate(ws.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=ws.max_row, values_only=True), 1):
    company = row[0]
    plant = row[1]
    total_mw = row[2]
    if company and plant and company != 'Company' and plant != 'Plant':
        if plant.strip() not in non_plant_entries:
            count += 1
            if count <= 50:
                print(f"Row {i}: {company} | {plant} | {total_mw}")

print(f"\nTotal rows with valid Company+Plant: {count}")
```

```python
import openpyxl

# Load the workbook
path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_task_1_b20lso4a/119_src_0.xlsx"
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(path)
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Final check - look at all data rows to ensure we haven't missed any plants
# A plant entry should have:
# - A company name in column A
# - A plant name in column B
# - Often a Total MW value in column C

all_entries = []
for i, row in enumerate(ws.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=ws.max_row, values_only=True), 1):
    company = row[0]
    plant = row[1]
    total_mw = row[2]
    
    # Skip empty rows and header rows
    if company == 'Company' and plant == 'Plant':
        continue
    
    # Look for rows with both company and plant
    if company and plant:
        all_entries.append((i, company, plant, total_mw))

print(f"Total entries with Company+Plant: {len(all_entries)}")

# Filter to actual plants (exclude metadata rows)
exclude = {'NEW', 'Units 5-6 steam', 'Utility Peaker', 'Peaking units', '(Hays Road Plant)'}
actual_plants = [e for e in all_entries if e[2].strip() not in exclude]

print(f"Actual plants (after filtering): {len(actual_plants)}")

# Check for duplicates
plant_names = [e[2].strip() for e in actual_plants]
unique_plants = set(plant_names)
print(f"Unique plant names: {len(unique_plants)}")

if len(plant_names) != len(unique_plants):
    print("WARNING: Duplicates found!")
    from collections import Counter
    counts = Counter(plant_names)
    for name, count in counts.items():
        if count > 1:
            print(f"  '{name}' appears {count} times")
else:
    print("No duplicates - all plants are unique")
```

```python
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook
from collections import defaultdict

# Load the workbook
file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_task_10_pzt37kk_/67_src_0.xlsx"
wb = load_workbook(file_path, data_only=True)  # Use data_only to get calculated values

# Read summary sheet
summary = wb['summary']

# Get all data from summary
print("=== Reading Summary Data ===")
data_rows = []
for i, row in enumerate(summary.iter_rows(min_row=8, values_only=True), 8):
    if row[0]:  # If Operator is not None
        operator = row[0]
        dollars = row[1]
        volume = row[2]
        date = row[3]
        imb_type = row[4]
        data_rows.append({
            'row': i,
            'operator': operator,
            'dollars': dollars,
            'volume': volume,
            'date': date,
            'imbal_type': imb_type
        })

# Get unique Imbal Types

# Read area info sheet
area_info = wb['area info']
print("\n=== Reading Area Info ===")
area_map = {}
for row in area_info.iter_rows(min_row=2, values_only=True):
    if row[0] and row[1]:
        operator = row[0].strip()
        area = row[1].strip()
        area_map[operator] = area

print(f"Area mappings: {len(area_map)}")
print("\nSample mappings:")
for i, (k, v) in enumerate(list(area_map.items())[:15]):
    print(f"  {k} -> {v}")

# Check which operators in summary have area mappings
print("\n=== Checking Operator Coverage ===")
matched = 0
unmatched = []
for r in data_rows:
    op = r['operator']
    if op in area_map:
        matched += 1
    else:
        unmatched.append(op)

print(f"Matched: {matched}, Unmatched: {len(unmatched)}")
print(f"Unmatched operators: {unmatched[:20]}")
```

```python
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook
from collections import defaultdict
from openpyxl.styles import Font, Alignment, Border, Side, PatternFill
from openpyxl.utils import get_column_letter

# Load the workbook
file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_task_10_pzt37kk_/67_src_0.xlsx"
wb = load_workbook(file_path, data_only=True)

# Read summary sheet
summary = wb['summary']

# Get all data from summary - filter for actual data rows
data_rows = []
for i, row in enumerate(summary.iter_rows(min_row=8, values_only=True), 8):
    operator = row[0]
    dollars = row[1]
    volume = row[2]
    date = row[3]
    imb_type = row[4]
    
    if operator and isinstance(operator, str) and imb_type in ['Dollar Valued', 'Volumetric']:
        data_rows.append({
            'row': i,
            'operator': operator.strip(),
            'dollars': dollars if dollars else 0,
            'volume': volume if volume else 0,
            'date': date,
            'imbal_type': imb_type
        })

# Read area info sheet
area_info = wb['area info']
area_map = {}
for row in area_info.iter_rows(min_row=2, values_only=True):
    if row[0] and row[1]:
        operator = row[0].strip()
        area = row[1].strip()
        area_map[operator] = area

# Match operators to areas
for r in data_rows:
    op = r['operator']
    if op in area_map:
        r['area'] = area_map[op]
    else:
        r['area'] = 'Unknown'

# Group by Imbal Type and Area
grouped = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))
for r in data_rows:
    grouped[r['imbal_type']][r['area']].append(r)

# Create the new worksheet
if 'by type_area' in wb.sheetnames:
    del wb['by type_area']
ws = wb.create_sheet('by type_area')

# Define styles
header_font = Font(bold=True)
header_fill = PatternFill(start_color='D3D3D3', end_color='D3D3D3', fill_type='solid')
total_font = Font(bold=True)
total_fill = PatternFill(start_color='E8E8E8', end_color='E8E8E8', fill_type='solid')
thin_border = Border(
    left=Side(style='thin'),
    right=Side(style='thin'),
    top=Side(style='thin'),
    bottom=Side(style='thin')
)

# Helper function to write table
def write_table(ws, start_row, title, imb_type, grouped_data):
    row = start_row
    
    # Title
    ws.cell(row=row, column=1, value=title)
    ws.cell(row=row, column=1).font = Font(bold=True, size=14)
    row += 2
    
    # Headers
    headers = ['Area', 'Volume', 'Value', 'Date']
    for col, header in enumerate(headers, 1):
        cell = ws.cell(row=row, column=col, value=header)
        cell.font = header_font
        cell.fill = header_fill
        cell.border = thin_border
        cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal='center')
    row += 1
    
    # Data rows
    areas = sorted(grouped_data.keys())
    total_volume = 0
    total_value = 0
    
    for area in areas:
        items = grouped_data[area]
        area_volume = sum(item['volume'] for item in items)
        area_value = sum(item['dollars'] for item in items)
        
        # Get the most recent date
        dates = [item['date'] for item in items if item['date']]
        latest_date = max(dates) if dates else None
        
        total_volume += area_volume
        total_value += area_value
        
        ws.cell(row=row, column=1, value=area).border = thin_border
        ws.cell(row=row, column=2, value=area_volume).border = thin_border
        ws.cell(row=row, column=2).number_format = '#,##0.00'
        ws.cell(row=row, column=3, value=area_value).border = thin_border
        ws.cell(row=row, column=3).number_format = '$#,##0.00'
        if latest_date:
            ws.cell(row=row, column=4, value=latest_date).border = thin_border
            ws.cell(row=row, column=4).number_format = 'YYYY-MM-DD'
        else:
            ws.cell(row=row, column=4, value='').border = thin_border
        row += 1
    
    # Total row
    ws.cell(row=row, column=1, value='TOTAL').font = total_font
    ws.cell(row=row, column=1).fill = total_fill
```

```python
import openpyxl
from datetime import datetime

# Load the workbook
file_path = '/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_task_3_uifwvic9/34_src_0.xlsx'
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(file_path, data_only=True)

# Get Flat sheet data
flat_sheet = wb['Flat']

# Row 18: Month dates (columns B-M are Jan-Dec 2002)
# Row 35: Total MWh per month
# Row 37: Value per month

# Jul-Dec 2002 are columns H-M (indices 7-12, 0-indexed)
# Let's get the data for Jul-Dec 2002

print("=== FLAT CONTRACTS (Jul-Dec 2002) ===")
flat_row_dates = list(flat_sheet.iter_rows(min_row=18, max_row=18, values_only=True))[0]
flat_row_mwh = list(flat_sheet.iter_rows(min_row=35, max_row=35, values_only=True))[0]
flat_row_value = list(flat_sheet.iter_rows(min_row=37, max_row=37, values_only=True))[0]

# Jul-Dec 2002: columns H-M (indices 7-12)
jul_dec_indices = [7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12]  # H, I, J, K, L, M
months = ['Jul', 'Aug', 'Sep', 'Oct', 'Nov', 'Dec']

flat_total_mwh_jul_dec = 0
flat_total_value_jul_dec = 0

for i, idx in enumerate(jul_dec_indices):
    month = months[i]
    date = flat_row_dates[idx]
    mwh = flat_row_mwh[idx] if flat_row_mwh[idx] else 0
    value = flat_row_value[idx] if flat_row_value[idx] else 0
    print(f"{month} 2002: Date={date}, MWh={mwh}, Value=${value:,.0f}")
    flat_total_mwh_jul_dec += mwh
    flat_total_value_jul_dec += value

print(f"\nFlat Total MWh (Jul-Dec 2002): {flat_total_mwh_jul_dec:,.0f}")
print(f"Flat Total Value (Jul-Dec 2002): ${flat_total_value_jul_dec:,.0f}")

# Calculate added value for Flat: $2/MWh increase
flat_added_value = flat_total_mwh_jul_dec * 2
print(f"Flat Added Value ($2/MWh increase): ${flat_added_value:,.0f}")

```

```python
import openpyxl
from openpyxl.worksheet.table import Table, TableStyleInfo
from openpyxl.utils import get_column_letter
from openpyxl.worksheet.filters import FilterColumn, Filters

# Load the workbook
file_path = "/var/folders/ff/24w7wvwj09jcc5jk_ch8rk6r0000gn/T/financial_env_task_6__ea0b_t8/60_src_0.xlsx"
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(file_path)

# Get the "All Natural Gas" sheet
sheet = wb["All Natural Gas"]

# Find all red-highlighted COUNTERPARTY values
red_counterparty_values = set()

for row_idx in range(1, sheet.max_row + 1):
    row_has_red = False
    for col_idx in range(1, sheet.max_column + 1):
        cell = sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx)
        if cell.fill and cell.fill.fgColor:
            rgb = cell.fill.fgColor.rgb
            if rgb and (rgb == "FFFF0000" or rgb == "FF0000"):
                row_has_red = True
                break
    if row_has_red:
        cp_cell = sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=2)
        cp_value = cp_cell.value
        if cp_value:
            red_counterparty_values.add(cp_value)

print(f"Found {len(red_counterparty_values)} red COUNTERPARTY values: {sorted(red_counterparty_values)}")

# The header row is row 3
header_row = 3
data_start_row = 4
data_end_row = sheet.max_row

# Find the last column with data
last_col = 1
for col_idx in range(1, sheet.max_column + 1):
    for row_idx in range(header_row, data_end_row + 1):
        if sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx).value:
            last_col = col_idx
            break

# Create table reference
start_cell = f"A{header_row}"
end_cell = f"{get_column_letter(last_col)}{data_end_row}"
table_ref = f"{start_cell}:{end_cell}"
print(f"Table reference: {table_ref}")

# Remove existing table if any
if sheet.tables:
    for table_name in list(sheet.tables.keys()):
        del sheet.tables[table_name]

# Create the table
tab = Table(displayName="AllNaturalGasTable", ref=table_ref)
style = TableStyleInfo(
    name="TableStyleMedium9",
    showFirstColumn=False,
    showLastColumn=False,
    showRowStripes=True,
    showColumnStripes=False
)
tab.tableStyleInfo = style
sheet.add_table(tab)

# Get all unique COUNTERPARTY values from data
all_cp_values = set()
for row_idx in range(data_start_row, data_end_row + 1):
    val = sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=2).value
    if val:
        all_cp_values.add(val)

print(f"Total unique CP values: {len(all_cp_values)}")

# Values to hide (non-red)
values_to_hide = [str(v) for v in all_cp_values if v not in red_counterparty_values]
print(f"Values to hide: {len(values_to_hide)}")

# Set up auto filter
sheet.auto_filter.ref = table_ref

# Create filter column for column B (index 1, 0-based)
filter_col = FilterColumn(colId=1)
filter_col.filters = Filters(filter=values_to_hide)

# Clear existing filter columns and add new one
sheet.auto_filter.filterColumn = [filter_col]

print("Table created and filter applied successfully")

# Save
wb.save(file_path)
print(f"Saved to {file_path}")
```
